ddls:
CREATE TABLE "community_areas" (
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each community area */,
  "community_area_name" TEXT /* Name of the community area */,
  "ca_index" INTEGER /* Index of the community area */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the community area */,
  "area_square_miles" REAL /* Area of the community in square miles */,
  "median_age" REAL /* Median age of the population */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographic latitude of the community area */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographic longitude of the community area */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("area_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "housing_stats" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each housing statistic record */,
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the community area */,
  "percent_of_housing_crowded" REAL /* Percentage of housing that is considered crowded */,
  "percent_households_below_poverty" REAL /* Percentage of households living below the poverty line */,
  "median_home_value" REAL /* Median value of homes in the community area */,
  "median_rent" REAL /* Median rent for housing units */,
  "vacancy_rate" REAL /* Percentage of housing units that are vacant */,
  "year_built" INTEGER /* Average year homes were built */,
  "homeownership_rate" REAL /* Percentage of households that own their homes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_housing_stats_area_id FOREIGN KEY ("area_id") REFERENCES community_areas ("area_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "unemployment_stats" (
  "unemployment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unemployment statistic record */,
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the community area */,
  "percent_aged_16_unemployed" REAL /* Percentage of individuals aged 16 and over who are unemployed */,
  "labor_force_participation_rate" REAL /* Percentage of the population aged 16 and over that is in the labor force */,
  "median_income_unemployed" REAL /* Median income of unemployed individuals */,
  "long_term_unemployment_rate" REAL /* Percentage of unemployed individuals who have been unemployed for 27 weeks or more */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unemployment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_unemployment_stats_area_id FOREIGN KEY ("area_id") REFERENCES community_areas ("area_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_stats" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education statistic record */,
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the community area */,
  "percent_aged_25_without_high_school_diploma" REAL /* Percentage of individuals aged 25 and over without a high school diploma */,
  "percent_aged_25_with_bachelor_degree" REAL /* Percentage of individuals aged 25 and over with a bachelor's degree */,
  "percent_aged_25_with_graduate_degree" REAL /* Percentage of individuals aged 25 and over with a graduate degree */,
  "high_school_dropout_rate" REAL /* Percentage of individuals aged 16-24 who are not enrolled in school and have not completed high school */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_education_stats_area_id FOREIGN KEY ("area_id") REFERENCES community_areas ("area_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic statistic record */,
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the community area */,
  "percent_aged_under_18_or_over_64" REAL /* Percentage of the population aged under 18 or over 64 */,
  "percent_aged_18_to_64" REAL /* Percentage of the population aged 18 to 64 */,
  "percent_male" REAL /* Percentage of the population that is male */,
  "percent_female" REAL /* Percentage of the population that is female */,
  "percent_non_white" REAL /* Percentage of the population that is non-white */,
  "median_household_size" REAL /* Median number of people per household */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_area_id FOREIGN KEY ("area_id") REFERENCES community_areas ("area_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "economic_data" (
  "economic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each economic data record */,
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the community area */,
  "per_capita_income" REAL /* Per capita income for the community area */,
  "hardship_index" INTEGER /* Hardship index representing the economic challenges faced */,
  "median_household_income" REAL /* Median household income */,
  "poverty_rate" REAL /* Percentage of the population living in poverty */,
  "gini_index" REAL /* Gini coefficient, a measure of income inequality */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Percentage of the labor force that is unemployed */,
  "average_commute_time" REAL /* Average commute time in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("economic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_economic_data_area_id FOREIGN KEY ("area_id") REFERENCES community_areas ("area_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "community_profiles" (
  "profile_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each community profile */,
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the community area */,
  "housing_stat_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the housing statistics */,
  "unemployment_stat_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the unemployment statistics */,
  "education_stat_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the education statistics */,
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the demographic statistics */,
  "economic_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the economic data */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the profile */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the profile was created */,
  "profile_description" TEXT /* A brief description or summary of the community profile */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("profile_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_community_profiles_housing_stat_id FOREIGN KEY ("housing_stat_id") REFERENCES housing_stats ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_community_profiles_education_stat_id FOREIGN KEY ("education_stat_id") REFERENCES education_stats ("education_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_community_profiles_unemployment_stat_id FOREIGN KEY ("unemployment_stat_id") REFERENCES unemployment_stats ("unemployment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_community_profiles_area_id FOREIGN KEY ("area_id") REFERENCES community_areas ("area_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_community_profiles_demographic_id FOREIGN KEY ("demographic_id") REFERENCES demographics ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_community_profiles_economic_id FOREIGN KEY ("economic_id") REFERENCES economic_data ("economic_id")
)
